Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix Blazor Server interactive components not loading in Azure

Azure Container Apps terminates TLS at the ingress and forwards
HTTP internally. Without UseForwardedHeaders(), the app sees the
internal HTTP scheme instead of HTTPS. This caused two problems:

1. Blazor Server's antiforgery middleware rejected the SignalR
   circuit negotiate request because the Origin header from the
   browser (https://…) didn't match the server-perceived scheme
   (http://…). The circuit never connected, so interactive
   components (FluentSearch, FluentSwitch) stayed as inert
   prerendered custom elements that the browser couldn't activate.

2. UseHsts() silently did nothing because the app thought it was
   serving HTTP, so the Strict-Transport-Security header was never
   sent.

Fixes:
- Add ForwardedHeadersOptions trusting X-Forwarded-For and
  X-Forwarded-Proto from any proxy (Azure CA's proxy IPs are
  not fixed, so KnownNetworks/KnownProxies are cleared).
- Call app.UseForwardedHeaders() as the very first middleware so
  all downstream middleware (antiforgery, HSTS, HTTPS redirect)
  see the correct scheme and client IP.

Also add sticky session affinity to the deployment workflow.
Blazor Server's SignalR circuit must stay on the same replica
for the browser session lifetime; without it, requests can hit
a replica that has no record of the circuit, silently breaking
all interactive components.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(p1): remove stock_quantity from insert/patch payload; improve email placeholder detection

- API (POST /api/admin/products): remove stock_quantity from insertData — the
  column does not exist in the products table, causing Supabase to return
  PGRST204 on every product create. Root cause of all 6 admin CRUD test failures.
- API (PATCH /api/admin/products/[id]): remove stock_quantity from allowedFields
  and the branch that coerces it, for the same reason.
- ProductForm: remove stock_quantity from form state, submit payload, and the
  Stock Qty input field so the UI no longer attempts to persist a non-existent
  column. Both will be re-enabled once the migration adds the column (P2).
  RESEND_KEY_IS_PLACEHOLDER that also detects the default re_your_* placeholder
  value. Both sendEmail and sendOrderConfirmationEmail now log an explicit,
  actionable warning (with a link to resend.com/api-keys) so the missing key is
  immediately visible in server logs on every checkout webhook.

Test results: 198 passed / 0 failed / 4 skipped (was 192/6/4)

Add Power Query stability diagnostics, CLI backward-compat fix, and crash isolation tests (#560)

* Log: McCauley, Nate, Hanna stability investigation planning

Stability Bug Investigation (2026-03-21T06-14-44Z)
- McCauley reviewed last 10 PRs, produced investigation sequence
- Nate mapped 45+ existing tests, identified serial workflow gaps
- Hanna reviewed COM patterns, validated cleanup correctness

Findings:
- Existing tests prove command-level correctness; gaps in serial workflows
- Serial Power Query create → refresh → modify → reopen sequence not tested
- Post-timeout recovery and workbook reopen paths not explicitly validated
- COM cleanup patterns are sound; no code changes needed

Recommendation: regression-test-first approach (baseline → edge → recovery)

Updated:
- Orchestration logs for McCauley, Nate, Hanna (3 files)
- Session log: stability investigation plan summary
- Agent history files with team updates

* Add serial workflow regression tests for timeout/recovery

Two new test suites validate multi-operation timeout recovery patterns:

ExcelBatchSerialWorkflowTests (3 tests):
- SerialWorkflow_TimeoutInMiddle_LaterOperationsFailFast
- SerialWorkflow_ReopenAfterTimeout_NewSessionWorks
- SerialWorkflow_MultipleTimeouts_AllFollowUpOperationsFailFast

SessionManagerSerialWorkflowTests (4 tests):
- SerialWorkflow_GetSessionAfterTimeout_ReturnsNullOrFailsFast
- SerialWorkflow_CreateSessionAfterTimeoutCleanup_SucceedsOnSameFile
- SerialWorkflow_ActiveSessionCount_RemainsAccurateThroughTimeout
- SerialWorkflow_MultipleSessionsOneTimeout_OthersUnaffected

All tests PASS, confirming current implementation handles serial
timeout recovery correctly. These serve as regression baselines for
the workflow patterns described in the v1.8.32 bug report.

Test patterns modeled:
- Operation A (success) -> Operation B (timeout) -> Operation C (attempt)
- Timeout + dispose -> immediate reopen same file
- Multiple sessions, one timeout -> others unaffected
- GetSession/CloseSession/CreateSession behavior after timeout

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Serial workflow regression tests (7 tests) all GREEN. Current control layer
(ComInterop/session/timeout) is healthy. Any remaining bug lives above this
layer in CLI daemon state, service routing, or workbook-specific patterns.

DECISION: Escalate investigation to CLI/daemon/service wrapper seams.
- Nate: Find first true RED regression above current control layer
- Cheritto: Trace wrapper seam for where bug can survive

Changes:

This is a team pivot after passing serial workflow controls. The control tests
validate existing correct behavior as regression baselines. Focus shifts from
timeout recovery to layer-above investigation.

* .squad: Merge serial regression investigation results (no RED found)

## Overview
Scribe: Consolidated investigation results for stability bug (excel-mcp-stability-bug-report.md).
Nate and Cheritto completed comprehensive regression testing across ComInterop and CLI layers.
All control tests PASSED; no RED regression found at current visibility.

  * Nate's test results: 7 ComInterop control tests all PASSED

  * Test infrastructure complete and tagged RunType=OnDemand

  * Recommended test seam pattern for future regression coverage

  * Investigation metadata, participants, outcomes, findings, next steps

BLOCKED on user workbook or more specific reproduction steps.
Options for future work:

commit.message:
fix: handle IFDRational and other EXIF types in sidecar JSON

piexif IFDRational values from JPEG EXIF data are not JSON-serializable.
Add a _MetaEncoder fallback that converts Rational-like objects (anything
with numerator/denominator) to float, preventing pre-decode failures on
images with rich EXIF metadata.

fix: trigger decode replenishment from status poll

_replenish_decode_buffer() was only called from jobs/claim (worker
requests) and image ingest.  When no distributed workers are actively
claiming, the pre-decoder stops receiving new work — even though the
machine is idle.

Now also triggered from _handle_status() which runs every ~1s from
dashboard polling, ensuring continuous decode progress.  The internal
5s throttle prevents excessive DB queries.

commit.message:
feat: adaptive resource-aware pre-decode scheduling

Replace fixed buffer threshold (in_flight < 100) with CPU and disk I/O
monitoring via psutil.  The pre-decoder now:

- Samples CPU% and disk busy% every 5s via ResourceSampler
- Feeds large batches when system is idle (CPU < 40%, disk < 50%)
- Reduces batch size under moderate load
- Stops feeding when CPU > 70% AND disk > 80% (except to maintain
  a minimum buffer of 20 to prevent worker starvation)
- Scales default decode threads from 2 to min(cpu_count - 2, 6),

fix: decode buffer stalls when cached-pending count exceeds threshold

The buffer check counted cached images with ANY pending module. With
4,661 cached images × ~6 modules each, cached_pending was always >> 100
so the buffer appeared 'full' and decoding stopped permanently.

Fix: measure decode queue depth (in-flight = queued - done - failed)
instead of cached-pending-image count. This tracks how much work the
pre-decoder has left, not how many cached images exist. The decoder
continuously feeds new batches as in-flight work drops below the
buffer size.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: demand-driven pre-decode scheduling with buffer replenishment

Replace the one-shot 'decode everything' approach with an incremental
demand-driven system that keeps a configurable buffer of decoded images
ahead of workers:

PreDecoder:
- Add feed() method for incremental batch submission (reuses executor)
- Track _queued_ids to avoid duplicate submissions
- Monitor thread no longer shuts down executor (reused by feed())

Server scheduling:
- Replace _auto_trigger_pre_decode() with _replenish_decode_buffer()
- Called on every jobs/claim (throttled to 5s intervals)
- Queries pending images, checks cache buffer level, feeds more when
  buffer drops below threshold (default 100, env IMGANALYZER_DECODE_BUFFER_SIZE)
- Images ordered by pending job count (most-needed first)

Bug fix:
- _handle_status() had pre_decode section as dead code after an early
  return statement — status now properly reports cache progress
- Progress bar shows overall cache fill (cached/total) instead of
  internal batch progress
